data.title: Block surges nearly 10% on S&P 500 inclusion
data.description:
Shares of Block (NYSE:XYZ) jumped nearly 10% in extended trading on Friday on the announcement that the fintech firm will be added to the constituents of the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500).

According to S&P Dow Jones Indices, Block will replace Hess (NYSE:HES) in the S&P 500, effective before the opening of trading on Wednesday, July 23. Hess (NYSE:HES) will be acquired by Chevron (CVX) in a deal that closed on July 18.

Block lost nearly 15% so far this year, compared to the 7% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Alphabet earnings, McGraw Hill IPO, Trump talks AI, and more robotaxi buzz
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - July 21

  • Volatility watch - Northern Dynasty Minerals (NAK) and Asset Entities (ASST) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Monogram Technologies (MGRM), Cogent Biosciences Inc (COGT), and Vigil Neuroscience (VIGL). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Intapp (INTA) and NextNRG (NXXT).
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Verizon (VZ), NXP Semiconductors (NXPI), and Domino's Pizza (DPZ).
  • IPO watch - The IPO calendar heats up with global education company McGraw Hill (MH), global consumer intelligence company NIQ Global Intelligence (NIQ), and insurance player Accelerant (ARX) all expected to start trading.
  • Dividend watch - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Caterpillar (CAT), Dell Technologies (DELL), Lowe's Companies (LOW), and Pfizer (PFE). 
  • All day - Axsome Therapeutics (AXSM) will host its Frontiers in Brain Health R&D Day. The event will highlight Axsome’s singular, innovative late-stage CNS pipeline.

 

Tuesday - July 22

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Coca-Cola (KO), Philip Morris International (PM), Texas Instruments (TXN), General Motors (GM), and Lockheed Martin (LMT).
  • 8:30 a.m. WTI crude July futures will expire. Crude oil futures have seen extra volatility over the last year on contract expiration dates.
  • 8:30 a.m. Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell gives welcome remarks before hybrid "Integrated Review of the Capital Framework for Large Banks Conference" hosted by the Federal Reserve
  • 1:00 p.m. Federal Reserve Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman will host a fireside chat to discuss effects of artificial intelligence on banks, businesses, and consumers

 

Wednesday - July 23

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Alphabet (GOOG), Tesla (TSLA), IBM (IBM), T-Mobile US (TMUS), AT&T (T), Chipotle (CMG), Hilton Worldwide (HLT), and General Dynamics (GD). Options trading implies a double-digit move for Alaska Air (ALK) and Viking Therapeutics (VKTX) after their earnings are released.
  • All day - President Trump is scheduled to give a speech on AI. Traders expect extra volatility for Bitcoin (BTC-USD), Ethereum (ETH-USD), Solana (SOL-USD), and XRP (XRP-USD) into the speech.
  • All day - Circana will release its June report on video game hardware and software sales. Sony's (SONY) PlayStation 5 was the best-selling hardware platform in May for both units and dollars.
  • All day - Faraday Future Intelligent (FFAI) will host a private reception at the Capitol Hill Club in Washington, D.C., to highlight U.S. innovation and alignment with current trade policy goals.
  • 10:00 a.m. The Atlanta Fed Business Inflation Expectations update will be released. The report is a monthly survey conducted by the Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta to assess firms' expectations of future inflation and their current business conditions.
  • 1:00 p.m. The National Retail Federation will hold a back-to-school webinar featuring insights on what to expect this shopping season.
  • 5:30 p.m. Tesla (TSLA) will hold its earnings conference call, during which Grok is expected to make an appearance. Elon Musk has said Tesla vehicles and the Optimus V3 humanoid robot are now equipped with the generative AI chatbot. Robotaxi news from Tesla (TSLA) could be relevant to companies such as Uber (UBER), Waymo (GOOG), WeRide (WRD), Pony AI (PONY), Zoox (AMZN), and Lucid Group (LCID).

 

Thursday - July 24

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Honeywell (HON), Union Pacific (UNP), Blackstone (BX), Intel (INTC), Deutsche Bank (DB), and Newmont (NEM). Options trading implies a double-digit move for IMAX (IMAX) and Boston Beer (SAM) after their earnings are released.
  • All day - Global flash PMI will be the focus of economists in an early gauge of the impact of the tariffs.
  • 7:45 a.m. The European Central Bank will release its monetary policy statement. ECB officials will also hold a press conference
  • 10:00 a.m. The New Home Sales report will be released by the U.S. Census Bureau.
  • 4:30 p.m. The Nasdaq short interest report will be released.

 

Friday - July 25

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include HCA Healthcare (HCA), Charter (CHTR), Phillips 66 (PSX), and Centene (CNC).
  • All day - The Alzheimer's Association International Conference will begin over the weekend in Toronto, Canada. It is the largest international conference dedicated to advancing dementia science. Some of the companies that will present include Acumen Pharmaceuticals (ABOS), Cerevance (CRVN), Marvel Biosciences (MRVL:CA), and Quanterix (QTRX).
  • 8:30 a.m. The June Durable goods order report will be released by the U.S. Census Bureau. Economists forecast a durable goods order decline of 10.0% for the month.

data.title: Linkage Global announces $3.5M convertible note financing
data.description:
• Linkage Global (NASDAQ:LGCB) announced on Friday that it has entered into a Securities Purchase Agreement for the issuance and sale of senior unsecured convertible notes in an initial principal amount of $3.5 million in a private placement.
  • The company expects to receive gross proceeds of $2.7 million at the initial closing. An additional $450,000 is expected to be funded within one business day following the effectiveness of a registration statement registering the resale of the underlying conversion shares.
  • The convertible notes are senior unsecured obligations of the company and were sold at 90% of their principal amount. They are convertible into the company’s Class A ordinary shares, par value $0.0025 per share.
  • The financing establishes a capital framework for additional issuances of up to $26.5 million in principal amount of similar convertible notes in future tranches. 
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for general corporate purposes, including expansion of its cross-border sales operations and integrated e-commerce service lines.
  • LGCB +0.51% after hours to $2.42.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Beneficient receives Nasdaq listing determination notice
data.description:
• Beneficient (NASDAQ:BENF) on Friday said it was notified by Nasdaq that, due to its continued non-compliance with the minimum $1.00 bid price requirement set forth in listing rules and the delay in the filing of Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended March 31, 2025 with the SEC, the company’s securities were subject to delisting unless the company timely requests a hearing before the Nasdaq Hearings Panel.
  • The company plans to timely request a hearing and a stay of any suspension action by Nasdaq, it said.
  • BENF -4.95% after hours to $0.3301.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Barnes & Noble Education delays annual report due to accounting errors
data.description:
Shares of Barnes & Noble Education (NYSE:BNED) cratered in late trading Friday after the company disclosed that the annual report for the fiscal year ended May 3, 2025, will be delayed due to an ongoing investigation regarding how the cost of digital sales were recorded.

The matter was brought to the attention of management in July, at which time the audit committee of the Board of Directors initiated an internal investigation.

Early findings indicate the company may have overstated accounts receivable by up to $23M, which will impact operations for the period ended May 3, 2025.  Total sales are not expected to have been impacted.

While it is unlikely the error will result in the restatement of previously issued financial statements, the company is looking into internal controls over financial reporting and expects to report at least one material weakness tied to manual journal entries.

This “material weakness” will likely lead to the conclusion that Barnes & Noble’s (NYSE:BNED) internal control over financial reporting was not effective, and disclosure controls and procedures were not effective during the fiscal years ended May 3 and April 27, 2024.

Shares fell as much as 15% in after-hours trading.

data.title: Xcel Energy unveils expansion plans in Texas, New Mexico
data.description:
Xcel Energy (NASDAQ:XEL) outlined plans Friday to expand power generation in Texas and New Mexico, aiming to add more than 5,100 MW of new capacity by 2030 to meet fast-rising electricity demand, including extensions of 521 MW existing generation and the addition of 17 new power projects.

Technologies selected include 3,200 MW of dispatchable generation and energy storage and 1,968 MW of wind and solar facilities, establishing the foundation for long-term system reliability, the company said.

Xcel (NASDAQ:XEL) said electricity demand in the region is forecast to grow by more than 40% by 2030, driven by population growth, industrial expansion and increased electrification, with new planning reserve margin requirements from the Southwest Power Pool - the grid operator for much of the region - also have increased capacity expectations during seasonal peaks.

The portfolio also includes extending the lives of existing natural gas units at Nichols, Maddox, and Plant X, the company said.

Xcel (XEL) said the expansion is part of its broader five-year infrastructure investment plan for Texas and New Mexico, announced in October, which aims to modernize and expand the region’s energy infrastructure.

data.title: Bowen Acquisition receives Nasdaq delisting notification
data.description:
• Bowen Acquisition (NASDAQ:BOWN) announced on Friday that it received a delisting determination letter, notifying the company that its securities are subject to delisting from Nasdaq.
  • The delisting determination letter stated that Bowen Acquisition was not in compliance with Nasdaq Listing Rules.
  • The company may appeal the staff’s determination to a Nasdaq Hearings Panel pursuant. Unless the company requests an appeal of Nasdaq’s determination on July 22, 2025, trading of its securities will be suspended at the opening of business on July 24, 2025, and Nasdaq will file a Form 25-NSE with the Securities and Exchange Commission to remove its securities from listing and registration on the Nasdaq Stock Market. 
  • The company said it intends to request a hearing before the panel to appeal the delisting determinations and to request a stay of the suspension of BOWN’s securities from trading.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Lyell Immunopharma registers 625,000 shares for resale
data.description:
• Lyell Immunopharma (NASDAQ:LYEL) registers 625,000 common shares for resale by selling stockholders.
  • Shares were issued as additional merger consideration under the October 2024 merger agreement with ImmPACT Bio.
  • Issuance triggered by achievement of a clinical milestone tied to ImmPACT Bio’s next-gen CAR T-cell product.
  • Milestone was met, and shares issued to stockholders on July 2, 2025.
  • Lyell will not receive any proceeds from the resale.
